hardware: AMD Strix Halo (Ryzen AI Max+ 395) iGPU gfx1151 / 96 CUs
model_class: Qwen3.5/3.6 35B-A3B MoE + MTP block (BF16 weights, FP16 attn-activation path)
purpose: Measure MTP decode tok/s for the small-batch dispatch split at sites #1 and #2 (project_full_attention_qkv_fp16, project_linear_attention_qkv_z_fp16). Mirrors the bf16 sibling pattern at project_linear_attention_qkv_z_bf16 line 1075+ where the multi-token path uses two single GEMVs instead of one dual GEMV to avoid row-stride aliasing with the qkv/z views.

workload.prompt_description: stable quicksort prompt from the 2026-05-19 persistent_b3 diagnostic

comparison.before_label: HIPENGINE_SMALL_BATCH_DECODE_THRESHOLD=1 (pre-M7.C.6 behavior; site #1/#2 fall to awq_fusedw4_prefill_dual_fp16 at tokens>1)
comparison.after_label: HIPENGINE_SMALL_BATCH_DECODE_THRESHOLD=7 (M7.C.6 active; site #1/#2 use two single GEMVs at 1<tokens<=7)

rocprofv3_kernel_breakdown_per_pass.comment: rocprofv3 trace shows the kernel switch worked mechanically: 30 awq_fusedw4_prefill_dual_fp16 calls (10 full_attn + 20 linear_attn — site #2 hits more layers than expected because some linear-attn layers may have multiple dispatches) are replaced by ~70 single gemv_awq_pack8 calls. Kernel-level deltas are small (~1 ms net) and within run-to-run noise; the real win is wall-time (rocprofv3 overhead dilutes signal).

correctness_gate.method: scripts/mtp_chain_e2e_smoke.py exact-AR-match: MTP output token-by-token equal to the AR baseline on the same prompt and seed; accepted-lengths pattern identical to pre-M7.C.6.
correctness_gate.fixture: quicksort prompt (90 tokens), 32 decode tokens, B=3, persistent_device proposer, c1_loop chain attention mode

code_changes.summary: Added `elif tokens <= _small_batch_decode_threshold():` branches at project_full_attention_qkv_fp16 (line 2002) and project_linear_attention_qkv_z_fp16 (line 3337) that issue two separate gemv_awq_pack8_transposed_fp16 calls — one for Q (writes scratch.q_proj.ptr) + one for K (writes scratch.key_bf16.ptr) at site #1, one for QKV (writes scratch.qkv.ptr) + one for Z (writes scratch.z.ptr) at site #2. Mirrors the bf16 sibling pattern at project_linear_attention_qkv_z_bf16 line 1075+. The `_small_batch_decode_threshold()` helper added in the M7.C.2 investigation is now actively used. Default threshold 7 (verifier B<=6 stays on the small-batch path).
architectural_invariants_preserved: Torch-free runtime — no torch import on the hot path
Plugin registry untouched — the dispatch lives where it already lived

Fused composite still has an unfused fallback (the existing tokens==1 dual GEMV is still the fast path; the threshold>=1 elif is a new mid-tier; the tokens>threshold prefill kernel is still the fallback)

plan_impact.m7c_phase_actual_reach_ms_per_pass: ~1 ms (within noise; real win is at the wall-time / cache-warmth level)
plan_impact.m7c_phase_projected_reach_revised_to: 1 ms kernel + 4 ms wall (cache + dispatch warmth)
plan_impact.explanation: The original 6-8 ms projection assumed prefill kernels were ~3-4x slower than GEMV at small batch. M7.0 + M7.C.6 actually measured prefill at ~123 us avg and GEMV at ~52 us avg per launch — the ratio is closer to 2.4x, not 3-4x. And the dual prefill writes 2 outputs in one launch while we need 2 GEMV launches to replace it. Net local savings come out to ~20 us per replaced site call, totaling ~1 ms. The 15.8% MTP tok/s improvement is mostly from cache-warmth effects (the dispatch is now better-shaped for the verifier's small-batch case).
